programme.desc: In Winnipeg, during the 1970s, is where self-made fashion mogul Peter Nygard got his start, and where survivors allege that he first begins assaulting young women.

programme.title: Cirque Life
programme.desc: Eric-the-clown's re-entry proves tricky for him and his understudy; a mandatory fitness evaluation sparks a friendly competition; and 'Stage sisters' Zore and Sarah perfect their new trick.

programme.title: Cirque Life
programme.desc: Nelson faces a shoulder injury with repercussions for his career and relationship; Zore and Sarah discover a new superpower; Eric meets his clowning mentor; questions swirl about life after Cirque.

programme.title: Rising Through the Fray
programme.desc: Indigenous Rising skates to claim their space on the roller derby track as the first borderless team in the sport.

programme.title: The Nature of Things
programme.desc: The human voice is a very sophisticated communication tool, but most lack knowledge of how to unlock its potential.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of American director David Lynch, whose films include Eraserhead, Mulholland Drive, Wild at Heart, Blue Velvet and The Elephant Man.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and career of screenwriter and director Preston Sturges, whose films include Sullivan's Travels and The Great McGinty, for which he won an Oscar for original screenplay.

programme.title: The Man Who Bought Cricket
programme.desc: The story of Allen Stanford's plan to revolutionise the game of cricket, which fell apart when he was exposed as a financial fraudster.

programme.title: The Man Who Bought Cricket
programme.desc: Now bankrupt, Allen Stanford spies a new opportunity in Montserrat. However, his new venture attracts the attention of US and UK law enforcement agencies.

programme.title: The Man Who Bought Cricket
programme.desc: Allen Stanford moves his operation to Antigua where he discovers the power of cricket in the Caribbean.

programme.title: Dogtown and Z-Boys
programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of skateboarding, from its 1970s emergence as a land-based form of surfing in Los Angeles and its subsequent explosion of popularity worldwide, to its current status as a widely-practised extreme sport. Narrated by Sean Penn, with contributions from experts Jay Adams and Tony Alva.

programme.title: My Icon: Thierry Henry
programme.desc: The former Arsenal and France forward speaks about the individuals that have been an inspiration to him over the course of his career.

programme.title: Bardot
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of actress Brigitte Bardot, examining the hardships, trials and struggles she faced as well as her work and legacy.

programme.title: I Am Burt Reynolds
programme.desc: Family and friends of the iconic Hollywood actor come together to share memories of his life and fruitful career, from its beginnings in Old Hollywood through to breakout roles in Deliverance and The Longest Yard, to the smash hit, Smokey and the Bandit and on to Boogie Nights.

programme.title: The Bee Gees: How Can You Mend a Broken Heart
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of the group, featuring contributions from surviving member Barry Gibb as well as archive interviews with his brothers Robin and Maurice.

programme.title: The Truth vs Alex Jones
programme.desc: Following the two defamation lawsuits brought by families of Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting victims against Alex Jones and his website InfoWars.

programme.title: Belushi
programme.desc: The life story of comedy star John Belushi, who captured the hearts and funny-bones of audiences worldwide before his tragic and untimely death aged 33.

programme.title: Undefeated
programme.desc: Documentary following an American football team from an underprivileged Memphis high school. The film tells the story of the local businessman who took over coaching the squad and hopes to turn their fortunes around, as well as three players who must balance training with their education as they strive to achieve college placements.

programme.title: The Unbreakable Tatiana Suarez
programme.desc: Documentary about the American professional mixed martial artist who competes in the Ultimate Fighting Championship.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and career of American film and TV director, producer and screenwriter James L Brooks, who won an Oscar for Terms of Endearment and also commissioned The Simpsons.

programme.title: Discovering: Claudia Cardinale
programme.desc: An intriguing profile of Tunisian-born Italian film actress Claudia Cardinale, who appeared in some of the most acclaimed European films of the 1960s and 1970s.

programme.title: Discovering: Walter Matthau
programme.desc: Film experts reflect on the life and career of Oscar-winning actor Walter Matthau, who starred in the comedies Fortune Cookie, The Odd Couple and Grumpy Old Men.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and career of producer and director Mervyn LeRoy, whose films include Little Caesar, Thirty Seconds Over Tokyo, Random Harvest, Quo Vadis and Mister Roberts.

programme.title: The Fog of War
programme.desc: Former US Secretary of Defense Robert McNamara looks back over his career, explaining why, after initially supporting the decision to go to war in Vietnam, he eventually chose to speak out against the conflict - a decision that cost him his position. Documentary by acclaimed film-maker Errol Morris.

programme.title: EFL: The Play-Offs at 40
programme.desc: Marking the 40th season since the play-offs were introduced for the 1986-87 campaign, this programme looks back at the history of the set of high-stakes fixtures.

programme.title: The Armstrong Lie
programme.desc: Documentary following cycling champion Lance Armstrong's fall from grace. Film-maker Alex Gibney was initially commissioned to follow the cyclist as he prepared to make a professional comeback by competing in the Tour de France, only to find himself documenting an unprecedented scandal as Armstrong was exposed as having used performance-enhancing drugs.

programme.title: Queen of Speed
programme.desc: A look at the career of French driver Michele Mouton, charting her battle to rise to the top of the male-dominated world of rallying in the 1970s and '80s.

programme.title: Pauline Black: A 2-Tone Story
programme.desc: Documentary tracing the Selecter frontwoman's career in music, as she discusses her experience of adoption, abuse, persona, gender and perseverance. With contributions from Arthur Hendrickson, Don Letts, Skin, Damon Albarn, Rhoda Dakar, Lynval Golding, Mykiell Riley, Sonia Boyce and Jools Holland.

programme.title: My Mom Jayne
programme.desc: Actress Mariska Hargitay explores the life of her mother Jayne Mansfield, who died in a car accident at the age of 34 when Mariska was only three years old. Through intimate interviews and a collection of never-before-seen photos and home movies, the Law & Order: Special Victims Unit star grapples with her mother's public and private legacy and discovers the layers and depth of who she was, not on

programme.title: Tell Them You Love Me
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the story of esteemed university professor Anna Stubblefield's controversial affair with Derrick Johnson, a non-verbal man with cerebral palsy.

programme.title: Bowling for Columbine
programme.desc: Michael Moore's Oscar-winning documentary exploring America's love affair with firearms. Going beyond public opinion, the film-maker examines corporate and political influence, visiting a bank where weapons are given to customers, as well as talking to Charlton Heston, one of the most famous proponents of every American's right to bear arms.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Wild City
programme.desc: The veteran broadcaster narrates a day in the animal life of Singapore's urban environments, from a troop of suburban macaques to a family of otters in Marina Bay.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Wild City
programme.desc: Exploring Singapore's islands, which have a host of remarkable creatures, including the tiger tail seahorse and the quick-changing cuttlefish.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Dragons and Damsels
programme.desc: Arguably more beautiful than butterflies, better fliers than hummingbirds and with intriguing and complex behavioural patterns - dragonflies and damselflies are marvels of the insect kingdom. This documentary uses the latest camera technology to show their manoeuvres, mating strategies, territorial behaviour and flight dynamics, revealing them to be the ultimate insect.

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Following his visit to the Great Barrier Reef in 1957, naturalist and broadcaster David Attenborough returns and uses the latest filming techniques to unlock the secrets of the natural wonder. In the first edition, he descends beneath the waves at night and uses cutting-edge technology to generate computer scans of the sea floor. He also learns about a complex community of animals, as well as the

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Using the latest technology, the naturalist explores the shark-infested waters of Osprey Reef in the Triton submersible, before returning to the nesting grounds of the Green Sea Turtle, 60 years after his first visit to Raine Island. He also marvels at manta ray cleaning stations on Lady Elliot Island, and tracks dwarf minke whales to discover their surprising reasons for returning, and why the re

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough

programme.title: David Attenborough Meets Obama
programme.desc: Another chance to see Barack Obama's conversation, recorded in May 2015, with renowned naturalist and film-maker David Attenborough. In a twist on usual convention, the then-US president took the role of interviewer, quizzing Attenborough about his thoughts on the future of the planet, their shared passion for nature, and potential ways to protect the environment. programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough employs the latest technologies to explore the violence, rivalries and deadly weaponry existing within the world of bugs. This first episode examines the survival tactics of its terrifying residents including killer ants, trap-setting spiders and beetles with the ability to shoot boiling chemicals at their enemies.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues his bug-eyed view of the world of creepy-crawlies, revealing how predators defuse the defences of their prey. Highlights include the cockroach wasp, busy preparing a tasty - and very live - treat for its young, the whirligig beetle, which employs a water-based radar system, and the jumping portia spider, which feeds on other arachnids.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A further selection of creepy-crawly close-ups, focusing on courtship in the bug world. Highlights include scorpions shaking their booty, the perfume-wearing nasonia wasp and the tropical cricket, for whom romance goes out of the window as it uses bribery to seal the deal with would-be partners. One potential partner to steer well clear of, however, is the praying mantis, the female of which is no

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A close-up view of sex, bug-style, as David Attenborough talks viewers through the different ways in which creepy-crawlies reproduce. programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough shifts the focus to bugs that prefer co-operation rather than conflict. They include burrowing cockroaches, the suitably named social spiders - which share a 30-metre web - the queen bee and her happy hive of workers and green ants pulling together to build a nest.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his close-up examination of the creepy-crawly world, shifting focus to the bugs' ultimate invention - the colony. Key residents of these minuscule mega-cities include honey bees, termites, leafcutter ants - who have a knack for farming - and their Argentine cousins, members of a community so vast it spans an entire continent.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Kingdom of Plants
programme.desc: The naturalist and film-maker explores the many varieties of plant life housed at the Royal Botanic Gardens in Kew, west London, and explains what they reveal about the natural world and the development of life on Earth. programme.title: David Attenborough's Kingdom of Plants
programme.desc: The naturalist explores how the plants at Kew Gardens change through the seasons and demonstrates the surprising properties of several species, including the predatory pond-dwelling bladderwort utricularia and a fungus that appears to be capable of mind control. He also discusses the largest single organism on the planet, visits the world's biggest flower and explains how plants and insects can co

programme.title: David Attenborough's Kingdom of Plants
programme.desc: The naturalist concludes his exploration of the Royal Botanic Gardens in Kew, west London, by examining plants that have evolved to minimise their dependence on water, enabling them to survive in the world's driest environments. programme.title: Flying Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the world of the pterosaur, a flying creature that occupied the skies 200 million years ago. programme.title: Making David Attenborough's Flying Monsters
programme.desc: Exploring the creation of David Attenborough's documentary, which uses state-of-the-art 3D technology to bring the airborne animals of the prehistoric world to life.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Rise of Animals: Triumph of the Vertebrates
programme.desc: Part one of two. programme.title: David Attenborough's Rise of Animals: Triumph of the Vertebrates
programme.desc: The naturalist concludes his two-part exploration of the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years by looking at the history of mammals. He traces their journey from humble origins as tiny nocturnal forest-dwellers to their astonishing explosion in diversity and domination of the world following the death of the dinosaurs.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Global Adventure
programme.desc: Documentary capturing some of the greatest filming sequences of David Attenborough's career, using previously unseen footage, from the deepest dives in a submersible on the Great Barrier Reef to watching a million bats take off in a cave in Borneo. programme.title: David Attenborough's Global Adventure: Home Planet
programme.desc: Further footage of David Attenborough's global wildlife adventures, from the depths of the Great Barrier Reef to the ancient rainforests of Borneo.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Global Adventure: The Rise of Nature
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the rise of life on Earth, from the emergence of the first creatures on seabeds hundreds of millions of years ago to the wildlife of today.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The crew kicks off a new waterside build, but a surprise call from the homeowners could change everything; with icy delays, a custom grill pit and help from Francis, this episode is packed with challenges, charm, and unexpected turns.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The builders turn one of the biggest barns they've ever saved into an even bigger home; they work through sweltering heat to transform the Ohio double-pen barn into a huge log home in Cave Spring, Ga.; Mark also visits a small log cabin.

programme.title: Second Chance Stage
programme.desc: After a bombshell reveal, the finalists perform in front of friends, family and industry leaders who could make their dreams come true.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters

programme.title: Ask This Old House
programme.desc: Installing a bathroom recessed cabinet; replacing a chimney that has been causing water damage to an indoor charcoal grill.

programme.title: Ask This Old House
programme.desc: Solving gutter water runoff; lead carpenter Lasana Harper talks about his journey into carpentry; replacing a ceiling fan.

programme.title: This Old House

programme.title: This Old House
programme.desc: Disconnecting the main power; the old bump out is demoed; the bathroom is tested for asbestos; a carpenter talks about working with Parkinson's disease; the removal of the vinyl siding reveals the original clapboards.

programme.title: This Old House
programme.desc: Pouring new foundations; a winding staircase replaces an old steep one; building a retaining wall; replacing the old water lines.

programme.title: This Old House

programme.title: Restored
programme.desc: Brett receives a blast from the past when an old high school friend asks him to help restore her 1905 Queen Anne Victorian bungalow; having lived in the house for 30 years, she has many unfinished renovation projects.

programme.title: Storied Spaces
programme.desc: From revitalized estates to a re-conceptualized farmhouse, property owners in Oregon, England, and Italy tackle the caretaking of magnificently sized, grand homes with unique histories and purposes.

programme.title: Storied Spaces
programme.desc: From England to the Dominican Republic and coastal California, adventurous families cultivated their homes with the same goal in mind: achieving a slower pace of life by using a blend of natural materials and indoor-outdoor living.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: Colorado Mountain House
programme.desc: Chip and Jo take "Fixer Upper" outside of Texas for the first time; getting creative with logistics and design challenges, they begin to transform a 1960s cottage into a retreat for the entire family.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: Colorado Mountain House
programme.desc: As their daughter prepares to head off to college, Chip and Jo race to finish their Colorado retreat, juggling construction timelines and final additions for a meaningful family reveal.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: Jodi and Dave are used to spending their vacations traveling in their RV with their three children, but are now looking for a getaway close to their home in Buffalo, N.Y.; they find a property along Lake Ontario in Niagara County, but it needs work.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A military family known for frequently relocating is looking for a lakefront property to act as a constant to keep them grounded; their waterfront getaway will be a spot where the kids can grow up making memories out on the water.

programme.desc: A New Jersey family of five looks for a lakefront getaway in Pennsylvania's Pocono Mountains - they want a property they can put their personal stamp on, but with a limited budget, they need all hands on deck to keep costs down.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A blended family of eight finds a vacation home on Lake Martin, Ala.; the property needs major renovations, but he's a contractor and is ready to get to work to help the family realize their lakeside living dreams.
